is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study investigates DHFR mutations in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Tanzanian children; inclusion required monoinfection with P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports Pfdhfr codons 51 (Ile), 59 (Arg), and 108 (Asn/Ser/Thr) — all curated target positions.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 1 gives n=44 total with mutation patterns and percentages; text states 75% (33/44) had 108-Asn+51-Ile+59-Arg, 4.5% (2/44) 108-Asn only, 13.6% (6/44) 108-Asn+59-Arg, 2.3% (1/44) 51-Ile+59-Arg. Per-codon frequencies extractable.

sub_country_location.evidence: Samples collected in the village of Magoda, Tanga region, north-east Tanzania — a named sub-national site.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: 'a second therapeutic study was undertaken in 1995' — single collection year, well within 3-year window.

african_field_samples.evidence: 44 children enrolled in Magoda, Tanga region, Tanzania; parasite DNA from their heparinized blood. Lab strains (K1, FC27, etc.) used only as controls.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical therapeutic/molecular study with newly collected field isolates and PCR-RFLP genotyping.

duplicate_risk.evidence: 'a second therapeutic study was undertaken in 1995 in the same area among the same population as in 1994' (Rønn et al. 1996) — potential overlap with the earlier 1994 study population.

supplement.evidence: All frequency data are in Table 1 and main text; no supplementary materials referenced.

assessment.summary: Primary study of 44 P. falciparum-infected children in Magoda village, Tanga region, north-east Tanzania (collected 1995), genotyping DHFR codons 51, 59, 108 (and 16, 164) by PCR-RFLP. Table 1 provides extractable per-pattern frequencies at sub-country resolution within a single collection year. Meets all criteria; note possible overlap with the 1994 study of the same population.
